label: Wide 2BR / 1 bath — master front-left, standard rear-left, common bath + kitchen across the rear-right, wide great room takes the front-right. Private left / public right zone split.

adjacencies.note: master and standard stack vertically along the left wall — master at the front, standard at the rear. Shared wall only, no door between them.
master bedroom door faces the great room (master's east wall faces great's west wall).

standard's east wall overlaps great room's west wall — hosts standard's door. 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + 0.15 m frame each side, keeping the door panel clear of the common bath's south wall when it swings open.
common is just east of standard at the rear; shared wall only, no door (single_bath means bath access through great room only).
common's south wall faces great room — door faces great for shared access by both bedrooms.
shared plumbing wall — kitchen and common bath stack rear-right; bath west of kitchen.
great room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style).

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_2br_kitchen_shape
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Floor kitchen width at 2.0 m on compact envelopes so the work triangle stays usable. Bedroom width floors are not needed — the solver's zone-ratio constraint (55/45 target, 50/50 floor) keeps the private wing balanced automatically.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology #1 — 2BR / 1 bath, wide analog of 1s_2br_sq_side_split_bath_gr.
Covers the 45-65 m² floor band on wide lots (buildable aspect ratio ≥ ~1.3).
Program: 2BR sharing ONE common bath. No ensuite. Both bedrooms reach the bath
through the great room. Entry is through the great room (street-facing front).
The 'side_split' name refers to the LEFT/RIGHT private-public zone split:
Private SIDE (left):  master (front-left) stacked above standard (rear-left).
Public SIDE (right):  great room (front-right) with kitchen open-plan at rear.
This is the same column-stack organisation as sq_side_split_bath_gr, ported to wide
shells where the bedrooms and great room gain horizontal width.
Structural difference from sq_side_split_bath_gr:
target_shell: 'wide' (only difference — all adjacency, anchoring, and stack
constraints are identical to the squarish sibling).
match_bedroom_widths is FALSE: standard must be wider than master by ≥ 1.2 m
so that standard's south wall overlaps the great room's north wall (hosting
standard's door). Equal widths would produce zero overlap and infeasibility.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_preference='right'):
Front: [master    | great_room                     ]
Rear:  [standard  | common | kitchen               ]
Standard extends EAST of master by ~1 m so its south wall overlaps the great
room's north wall — that overlap hosts standard's door to the great room.
Carport: 3.0 m wide × 6.0 m deep (Item 2 default). Note: the 1 m × 4 m
building_void at front-right requires ≥ 7 m buildable depth for standard to
have ≥ 3 m depth in the rear zone; use NCP briefs on 10 m-deep lots and
carport briefs on 11 m-deep lots (14 × 11 m lot → 10 × 7 m buildable).
When brief.carport_preference='left', the runner mirrors the topology.
When brief.carport_preference='none', the void is stripped and the footprint
is a clean rectangle — standard gains full shell depth.
